entities.name: āśraya
entities.cite: it exists only asrita, resting on its substrate

entities.name: guṇa
entities.cite: the guna is the prakarsa-hetu, the ground of the 'more'

axioms.name: prakarsa_hetu
axioms.cite: the guna is the prakarsa-hetu, the ground of the 'more'

axioms.name: svatantryena
axioms.cite: grammar treats it svatantryena, as if it were a free-standing item

axioms.name: asrita
axioms.cite: it exists only asrita, resting on its substrate

denials.name: svatantra
denials.cite: though in reality it exists only asrita, resting on its substrate

claims.name: prakarsa_hetu
claims.cite: The quality that is the ground of pre-eminence

rejected_readings.label: truly_independent_quality
rejected_readings.rendering: The quality that is the ground of pre-eminence is truly independent.
rejected_readings.why: Asserts the quality is truly independent (svatantra), contradicting the commentary's explicit statement that in reality it 'exists only asrita, resting on its substrate'. Grammar provisionally treats it as independent for analysis, but ontologically it is dependent.

rejected_readings.label: inverted_dependence
rejected_readings.rendering: The substrate rests on the quality; the quality is the base on which the substrate depends.
rejected_readings.why: Reverses the asrita relation: the quality (guna) is what rests on the substrate (asraya), not vice versa. The analytical prominence of the quality in grammar (svatantryena treatment) does not reflect its metaphysical dependence on the substrate.

notes: This verse exemplifies a central methodological principle: grammar provisionally abstracts ontologically dependent entities (qualities) as independent analytical units, enabling systematic rules without metaphysical commitment. The asrita relation (quality dependent on substrate) grounds the actual order; the prakarsa_hetu predication (quality as ground of pre-eminence) explains its analytical relevance.
